Triggering the system-level commercial city space restriction and '6-employee weak current security contractor/storefront' physical demotion probe. The illusory Apple consumer electronics OEM template in the original data has been completely cleared to zero. This enterprise, named 'Jingpin Electronic Technology,' was categorized by the official system into 'Electronic Device Manufacturing.' Its business license is filled with 'production and processing of electronic products, security products, and computers.' However, the forensic logic engine directly penetrated this extremely cheap disguise: the insured headcount is 6 people, and the paid-in capital is 211,400 RMB. The most fatal blow comes from its highly unique spatial coordinates: 'Room 10-182, Zone B, Nanjing Yiwu Small Commodity City, No. 388 Tianyuan East Road.' The logical deduction instantly turned into dark humor: in a storefront (Room 10-182) within a bustling 'Small Commodity City,' a boss with 5 buddies, carrying about 200k in their pockets, claims to have built a modern industrial assembly line capable of simultaneously producing electronic components, computers, and security surveillance equipment? The probe mercilessly deleted all 'production, processing' delusions from the license, extracting the actual work it can do in the small commodity city: 'sales of security products, electronic products; design and construction of network engineering and security engineering.' The truth is extremely grassroots: this is a 100% pure 6-Employee Commodity Market Store & Weak Current Contractor (also an electronic grocery shop). The boss rented a shop in the small commodity city, usually selling some electronic components, computer accessories, or surveillance cameras. If someone needs network cabling or surveillance installed (i.e., weak current engineering), the boss takes a few buddies from the store with toolboxes to the site for 'construction and installation.' The 'Electronic Device Manufacturing' tag bestowed by officials is the most down-to-earth mockery of the modern industrial system.
